prompt:
Using the pharmaversesdtm CDISC Pilot Study SDTM domains, derive an ADaM
Subject-Level Analysis Dataset (ADSL) for a three-arm parallel group study
(Xanomeline High Dose, Xanomeline Low Dose, Placebo).

Age grouping cut-points: <65 / 65-80 / >80.
Numeric treatment codes: Xanomeline High Dose = 3, Xanomeline Low Dose = 2,
Placebo = 1.
SAFFL: received at least one dose (EXDOSE > 0 or EXTRT contains "PLACEBO")
with non-missing EXSTDTC.
ITTFL: randomised — ARMCD != "Scrnfail" and ARM != "Screen Failure".

Include at minimum: STUDYID, USUBJID, SUBJID, SITEID, AGE, AGEU, SEX, RACE,
ETHNIC, COUNTRY, AGEGR1, AGEGR1N, TRT01P, TRT01PN, TRT01A, TRT01AN,
TRTSDTM, TRTSTMF, TRTEDTM, TRTETMF, TRTSDT, TRTEDT, TRTDURD, RANDDT,
RFSTDT, RFENDDT, EOSSTT, EOSDT, DCSREAS, DTHFL, DTHDT, LSTALVDT,
SAFFL, ITTFL.
expected_output:
Executable R code producing an ADSL dataset with one record per USUBJID
(306 subjects) that matches pharmaverseadam::adsl on key variables
(TRTSDT, TRTEDT, TRT01P, TRT01A, EOSSTT, SAFFL).
files: Input data loaded directly from the `{pharmaversesdtm}` R package (CDISC
Pilot Study). Reference output available from `{pharmaverseadam}` R package.
